title: Python for Data Analysis
description: Use pandas to load, clean, and summarize real-world CSV data. We'll go from a 200MB messy export to a chart-ready DataFrame in seven short lessons.

lessons.title: Reading CSVs without rage
lessons.content: pd.read_csv has forty-two parameters. You only need six.

lessons.title: Indexing, loc, and iloc
lessons.content: Use loc for labels, iloc for positions. Never chain [] on a DataFrame.

lessons.title: groupby in anger
lessons.content: Split, apply, combine. The three verbs are enough.

lessons.title: Merging and joining
lessons.content: merge is the vlookup of dataframes. on, left_on, right_on.

lessons.title: Quick plots with matplotlib
lessons.content: df.plot() is fine for an internal dashboard. Not for a paper.

lessons.title: Reading from a database
lessons.content: pd.read_sql is just read_csv with extra steps.

tasks.title: Clean a sales CSV
tasks.question: Given a CSV with mixed date formats, return a tidy DataFrame.
tasks.instruction: Use pd.to_datetime with format='mixed'. Drop rows with null totals.
